decision_point: In an adult with single-level cervical radiculopathy without progressive deficits, is anterior cervical discectomy and fusion (ACDF) or physical therapy preferred?
clinical_vignette:
Typical adult patient for whom this decision arises (no atypical comorbidities or contraindications).

Decision: In an adult with single-level cervical radiculopathy without progressive deficits, is anterior cervical discectomy and fusion (ACDF) or physical therapy preferred?
Options:
  1. Physical therapy and traction
  2. Anterior cervical discectomy and fusion
label: settled
label_rationale: Without progressive deficit, long-term functional outcomes converge between ACDF and structured nonoperative care; surgery offers faster early relief but adds exposure risk and adjacent-segment disease. Nonoperative-first is the settled standard, ACDF reserved for refractory or progressive cases (MD adjudication 2026-06-27; reproducibly converged at N=3).
